---
title: "Aggregate plot hPu hPy OKseq"
output: 
date: "2026-02-20"
---

######################################################################################################
# Gustave Roussy (GR) | CNRS UMR 9019, Genome dynamics and maintenance Lab - Gabriel MATOS RODRIGUES 
# Developed by BEKKOUCHE Louisa - bekkouche.louisa@gmail.com                                 
# Edited by: Genome dynamics and instability Lab                                     
# v.0.1 - 2025/12/18 (for Human genome) | v.0.2 2026/02/16 (for Mouse genenome)	     
# R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31)                                                       
# MIT licence - Copyright (c) 2025 Gabriel Matos Rodrigues			                     
# run it in a env conda for R version and enjoy your results! :)                     
#######################################################################################################

```{r}

#######################################################################################################

# Aggregate profiles from BigWig signals over BED regions
# Produces a line plot comparing conditions (e.g., pos.bw vs neg.bw)
# Dependencies: IRanges, GenomicRanges, GenomeInfoDb, rtracklayer, EnrichedHeatmap,
#               ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr

#######################################################################################################

# Packages installation CRAN + BIOCONDUCTOR


if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("BiocManager", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
}

cran_pkgs <- c("ggplot2","dplyr","tidyr")
bioc_pkgs <- c("IRanges","GenomicRanges","GenomeInfoDb","rtracklayer","EnrichedHeatmap")

# Utility function: installs only missing packages
install_missing_cran <- function(pkgs) {
    missing <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())]
    if (length(missing) > 0) {
        install.packages(missing, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
    }
}

# Install missing CRAN packages
install_missing_cran(cran_pkgs)
BiocManager::install(bioc_pkgs, ask = FALSE, update = TRUE)

message("All required packages are installed.")

library(IRanges)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(GenomeInfoDb)  
library(rtracklayer)    
library(EnrichedHeatmap)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

#######################################################################################################

# User inputs (edit these paths) 
bigwig_dir <- "/bw/path"
bed_path   <- "/bed/path"

# Validate BigWig files
bigwig_files <- list.files(bigwig_dir, pattern = "\\.(bw|bigWig)$", full.names = TRUE)
stopifnot("No BigWig" = length(bigwig_files) > 0)

# Derive labels from file names (without extensions)
file_basenames <- basename(bigwig_files)
file_labels    <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(file_basenames)  

# Import BED file and build GRanges
# Expecting BED with columns: chrom, start, end. Extra columns are ignored.
bed <- read.table(bed_path, header = FALSE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
stopifnot(ncol(bed) >= 3)
regions <- GRanges(seqnames = bed[[1]], ranges = IRanges(start = bed[[2]], end = bed[[3]]))

# Harmonize seqlevels style between regions and BigWig data
# Import one BigWig to capture its seqlevelsStyle
bw0 <- import(bigwig_files[1], format = "BigWig")
seqlevelsStyle(regions) <- seqlevelsStyle(bw0)

# Build signal matrices around regions for each BigWig
# normalizeToMatrix() from EnrichedHeatmap computes a matrix of signal around regions.
# Parameters: extend = 1000bp upstream and downstream, bin width w = 25bp.
signal_matrices <- lapply(bigwig_files, function(bw_file) {
  # Import signal track
  bw_data <- import(bw_file, format = "BigWig")
  # Harmonize chromosome naming style with regions
  seqlevelsStyle(bw_data) <- seqlevelsStyle(regions)
  # Create matrix of signal around regions
  normalizeToMatrix(
    bw_data,
    regions,
    value_column = "score",
    extend = 1000,        # extension
    mean_mode = "absolute",
    w = 25
  )
})

# Aggregate profiles: column means across regions for each BigWig
aggregate_profiles <- lapply(signal_matrices, function(mat) {
  # colMeans gives the average signal per bin across all target regions
  colMeans(mat, na.rm = TRUE)
})

# Prepare a tidy data frame for plotting 
# Combine aggregated profiles into a single data.frame
profile_df <- as.data.frame(do.call(cbind, aggregate_profiles))
colnames(profile_df) <- file_labels  

# Derive a Position axis matching the number of bins (from -1000 to +1000)
n_bins <- nrow(profile_df)
profile_df$Position <- seq(-1000, 1000, length.out = n_bins)

# Reshape to long format for ggplot
plot_data <- profile_df |>
  pivot_longer(cols = -Position, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Signal")

# Map Samples to original basenames and create a Condition column based on filename patterns
label_map <- data.frame(
  Sample = file_labels,
  Basename = file_basenames,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

plot_data <- plot_data |>
  left_join(label_map, by = "Sample") |>
  mutate(
    Condition = case_when(
      grepl("pos\\.bw$", Basename, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "pos.bw",
      grepl("neg\\.bw$", Basename, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "neg.bw",
      TRUE ~ "other"
    )
  )


# Define the plot colors per Condition
custom_colors <- c("pos.bw" = "black", "neg.bw" = "grey")


# Aggregate plot
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Position, y = Signal, color = Condition, group = Sample)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5, alpha = 0.9, linetype = "solid") +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, labels = c("Neg", "Pos")) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "hPu/hPy repeat center",
    y = "Normalized S1-END-seq (RPM)",
    color = "Strand"
  ) +
  ylim(-1.2,1.2) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 8))
  

print(p)

# Save the plot to disk
# provide a full filename including extension (e.g. .png or .pdf)
ggsave(
  filename = "/output/folder/path",
  plot = p,
  width = 3, height = 3, dpi = 100
)

#######################################################################################################
```
